calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
James E. Wager-Smith, Esq. for Appellant/Defendant Tel.(925)926-0202, for Hossein Daniel Aslani, DDS who is present.
Edwin J. Zinman, Esq. for Respondent/Plaintiff Tel.(415) 391-5353 for Crystal H. Casanave, who is present. Substitution of Attorney filed today.

This cause came on reguarly for Trial De Novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Mr. Zinman calls Hossein Daniel Aslani, DDS as an adverse witness. The following exhibits are identified and admitted into evidence:

Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #1: Diagram of Upper and Lower Teeth
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #2: Clear plastic bag containing actual crown of plaintiff
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #3: Panoramic X-ray of Ms. Casanave's mouth
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #4: Clear plastic bag containing empty medicine bottle of
Hydrocodone for plaintiff
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #6: Implant Consent Form and Patient Information
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #7: Restoration Consent Form of Dental Implants
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit #8: Dr. Watkin's Notes (3 pages)
Respondent/Plaintiff's Exhibit : Dr. Kevin B. Duquette letter and billing statement (3 pages)

Mr. Zinman calls the plaintiff Crystal Casanave to testify. She is cross-examined by Mr. Wager-Smith.

The Court reminds the Appellant/Defendant's counsel to manually file the Declaration of Standard Care from the expert testimony in Room 103. It is currently attached to his Trial Brief and was submitted to the Court.

Mr. Zinman calls David Casanave to testify on the stand. No further questions from Mr. Wager-Smith.

Mr. Zinman and Mr. Wager-Smith present closing arguments respectively.
Mr. Wager-Smith has no rebuttal. The Court informs counsel she will not accept any supplemental briefing for this matter.

The matter is submitted.

After the matter was submitted, the Court indicated that the Respondent/ Plaintiff could not prevail on her professional negligence claim as a matter of law because she failed to adduce any expert evidence that the appellant did not meet the standard of care. The Court indicated she would review the exhibits and other evidence relating to the failure to give implied consent claim and the applicable CACI instructions cited by counsel for respondent before issuing her decision. She informed counsel she would issue her ruling in a few days.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet, Clerk: Rosallie Gumpal, Not Reported, (624).
